title: Service Outcomes, Node 24 LTS, and the TypeScript Toolchain · Compiler Case: Node Type Stripping Boundary
summary: A community-service API counts routes as value, floats Node and npm versions, confuses TypeScript type erasure with validation, and cannot reproduce its build or startup decision. Planned artifact: Add and verify the httpsts node type stripping boundary increment in outcome, runtime, compiler, and reproducibility charter. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Use Node type stripping only for erasable syntax while keeping type checking, path transformation, module resolution, and production emission explicit.
Separate the TypeScript 7 native CLI from the TypeScript 6 compatibility API and prove which tool owns checking, emission, editor APIs, and build gates.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Node and Express server Predict Node Type Stripping Boundary · Theory Node Type Stripping Boundary
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory team handles case 8575 in a multi-tenant case service with a bounded PostgreSQL pool. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to gate a reproducible Node and TypeScript artifact instead of trusting local residue; keep rollback safe during a storage expansion; then change the tenant and prove denial before repository access. Browser code models pure decisions only. Node, Express, listener, TLS, PostgreSQL, proxy, worker, load, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates.
